message:
feat: configurable click interval, injectable AppendLogReader max payload, and tuning Eq derives

Configurable click interval (config.rs, input.rs, mouseevent.rs, user guide):
- Add click_interval_ms config field (default 500 ms, validated > 0)
  controlling the maximum time between successive clicks for double-click
  and triple-click selection detection.
- LastMouseClick::add() now accepts click_interval_ms parameter instead
  of using a hardcoded 500ms constant, and TermWindow passes the config
  value through.
- input.rs: update click timing logic to use the provided interval.
- docs/frankenterm-gui-user-guide.md: document click_interval_ms in the
  config table and add an "Accessibility Timing" section explaining how
  operators can raise it to 1000-2000 for slower cadences.

Injectable AppendLogReader max payload (tantivy_ingest.rs):
- Add max_record_payload_bytes field to AppendLogReader, replacing the
  inline const MAX_RECORD_PAYLOAD with self.max_record_payload_bytes.
- Add _with_max_record_payload variants for open(), open_at_ordinal(),
  and open_at_offset() so callers can inject the limit from TuningConfig
  rather than relying on the compiled default.
- The zero-argument open/open_at_* methods delegate to the _with
  variants using IngestTuning::DEFAULT_MAX_RECORD_PAYLOAD_BYTES.

LexicalIndexerConfig builder (recorder_lexical_ingest.rs):
- Add LexicalIndexerConfig::from_tuning_config() that constructs the
  indexer config from live TuningConfig fields, centralizing the mapping
  between tuning parameters and indexer initialization.

daemon_integration test updates:
- Import PersistedCapture and wire persist_captured_segment through a
  local wrapper that passes TEST_MAX_PERSIST_SEGMENT_BYTES, matching the
  updated function signature.

TuningConfig polish (tuning_config.rs):
- Add Eq derive to 9 tuning structs (SnapshotTuning, IngestTuning,
  PolicyTuning, AuditTuning, WebTuning, CassQueryConfig, WorkflowsTuning,
  SearchTuning, WireProtocolTuning, IpcTuning, WeztermTuning) — all
  fields are integer/bool/String types so Eq is trivially sound.
- Fix doc comment wording on IngestTuning fields.

Formatting (ingest.rs, config.rs):
- Rustfmt reformatting of persist_captured_segment call sites.
- Rustfmt import reordering in Config (split non-lua and lua-only imports).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -140,6 +140,7 @@ Current default key set from `crates/frankenterm-gui/frankenterm.toml`:
 | `initial_cols` | `120` | window startup columns |
 | `window_decorations` | `"TITLE | RESIZE"` | OS-dependent behavior |
 | `window_close_confirmation` | `"NeverPrompt"` | set stricter confirmation if desired |
+| `click_interval_ms` | `500` | accessibility: raise to `1000`-`2000` for a slower double-click cadence |
 | `[window_padding].left/right/top/bottom` | `4` | pixel padding around terminal viewport |
 | `enable_tab_bar` | `true` | show/hide tab bar |
 | `hide_tab_bar_if_only_one_tab` | `true` | keeps single-tab window clean |
@@ -173,6 +174,10 @@ SSH domain fields (optional per entry):
 - `connect_automatically`
 - `default_prog`
 
+### Accessibility Timing
+
+`click_interval_ms` controls how much time FrankenTerm allows between successive clicks when deciding whether a gesture counts as a double-click or triple-click selection. The default is `500`, which matches common desktop defaults, but operators who need a slower cadence can raise it to `1000`-`2000` in `frankenterm.toml`.
+
 ## Migration Guide: WezTerm -> FrankenTerm
 
 ### A. Config migration (Lua -> TOML)
